settings.title: Certified for Better Sleep
settings.text: When it comes to your health and comfort, we don’t cut corners. That’s why many BEDGEAR products and materials are backed by leading certifications you can trust.

settings.title:
GREENGUARD
Gold Certified
settings.description: Our crib mattresses are GREENGUARD Gold Certified, meaning they have been independently tested to meet rigorous limits for low chemical emissions, helping reduce indoor air pollution and support a healthy environment.

settings.tagline: Gentle on skin. Tough on toxins.
settings.title:
OEKO-TEX®
Standard 100
settings.description: The OEKO-TEX® Standard 100 certification on our sheets keeps you and your family safe by testing each component of the certified product against a list of over 1,000 harmful substances.

settings.title:
CertiPUR-US®
Certified Foam
settings.description: The foams in our mattresses are CertiPUR-US® certified, meaning they're made without ozone depleters, heavy metals, formaldehyde, or phthalates. They also have low VOC emissions, ensuring better indoor air quality.

settings.title: Sustainable Forestry Initiative®
settings.description: BEDGEAR cardboard packaging is sourced from suppliers certified by the Sustainable Forestry Initiative® (SFI®), supporting sustainable forest management practices that protect water quality, wildlife habitat, and long-term forest health.

settings.title:
Forest Stewardship
Council®
settings.description: BEDGEAR cardboard packaging is sourced from suppliers certified by the Forest Stewardship Council® (FSC®), supporting responsible forest management that protects biodiversity, ecosystems, and the rights of local and Indigenous communities.

settings.title:
Airflow
Certified®
settings.description: "Air Flow Certified" indicates this product has been laboratory tested for air permeability using ASTM D737 and meets our established minimum airflow performance thresholds.

custom_css: .benefits-header {max-width: 722px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 24px; text-align: center; font-variant-ligatures: none;}
certification_benefits_Td8ixQ.name: Certifications Benefits

settings.heading: Why Certifications Matter
settings.description: BEDGEAR meets rigorous standards for safety, sustainability, and quality. Choosing certified materials means fewer harmful chemicals and allergens, leading to a cleaner, healthier sleep environment.

settings.text: Share information about your brand with your customers. Describe a product, make announcements, or welcome customers to your store.

settings.question: What is the meaning of GREENGUARD?
settings.answer: GREENGUARD is a certification that ensures products meet strict chemical emissions limits, especially for indoor use. It verifies that a product contributes to healthier indoor air by emitting low levels of volatile organic compounds (VOCs).

settings.question: What does GREENGUARD mean?
settings.answer: It means the product has been tested for over 10,000 chemicals and VOCs and meets rigorous standards for low chemical emissions, helping reduce indoor air pollution.

settings.question: What is the difference between GREENGUARD and Green Seal?
settings.answer:
GREENGUARD focuses on indoor air quality and chemical emissions. 

Green Seal is broader, evaluating a product’s overall environmental impact, including manufacturing, packaging, and performance. 

So, GREENGUARD = cleaner air; Green Seal = greener footprint.

settings.question: How important is GREENGUARD certification?
settings.answer: Very important — especially for sleep products used in enclosed spaces like bedrooms. It gives peace of mind that the product won't off-gas harmful chemicals into your home, making it a smart choice for families and those with allergies or sensitivities.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD mean no formaldehyde?
settings.answer: It means very low formaldehyde emissions. While GREENGUARD doesn’t guarantee “zero,” certified products must meet strict limits well below typical indoor safety thresholds.

settings.question: Is GREENGUARD safe?
settings.answer: Yes. GREENGUARD-certified products are considered safe for indoor use, having passed stringent third-party testing to ensure low emissions of harmful chemicals.

settings.question: Is GREENGUARD Gold actually safe?
settings.answer: Yes — in fact, GREENGUARD Gold is even more rigorous than standard GREENGUARD certification. It’s designed for sensitive environments like schools and healthcare settings, making it ideal for homes.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD Gold allow formaldehyde?
settings.answer: It allows only extremely low levels. Products must emit less than 9μg/m³ of formaldehyde — a fraction of the limits allowed by standard building codes.

settings.question: What is GREENGUARD made of?
settings.answer: GREENGUARD is a certification, not a material. It applies to finished products — like mattresses, furniture, or fabrics — ensuring they emit minimal VOCs.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD have PFAS?
settings.answer: GREENGUARD certification does not specifically test for PFAS. However, products that pass testing must meet strict overall VOC emission limits, which can include some PFAS-related compounds.

settings.question: Is GREENGUARD Gold low in VOC?
settings.answer: Yes. GREENGUARD Gold is among the most stringent low-VOC certifications available, helping ensure safer air in your home.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD mean low VOC?
settings.answer: Absolutely. The core purpose of GREENGUARD is to guarantee low chemical emissions, including VOCs, into indoor air.

settings.question: Is GREENGUARD low in VOC?
settings.answer: Yes. Products with GREENGUARD or GREENGUARD Gold certification are verified to emit very low levels of VOCs, which can help reduce headaches, allergies, and chemical sensitivities.

settings.question: What does it mean to be GREENGUARD certified?
settings.answer: It means the product has passed independent laboratory testing for chemical emissions and contributes to better indoor air quality. It’s a mark of trust for health-conscious consumers.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD mean non-toxic?
settings.answer: GREENGUARD certification supports non-toxic goals by limiting harmful emissions, but it doesn't test every potential toxic ingredient. It’s best paired with other certifications like OEKO-TEX for broader chemical safety.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD really matter?
settings.answer: Yes — especially for products used in the home daily. GREENGUARD helps reduce your exposure to indoor pollutants that can affect respiratory health and overall well-being.

settings.question: What is low VOC considered?
settings.answer: For GREENGUARD, low VOC typically means emissions are within strict thresholds (e.g., formaldehyde under 9μg/m³ for Gold level). These limits are set to minimize health risks from indoor air exposure.

settings.question: Is Room & Board GREENGUARD certified?
settings.answer: Some Room & Board products may carry GREENGUARD certification. It's best to check their specific product listings or contact the company for verification.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD Gold mean no formaldehyde?
settings.answer: It means extremely low formaldehyde emissions. Not entirely “zero,” but well below most safety guidelines — and far lower than what’s found in many conventional products.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD Gold certified mean non-toxic?
settings.answer: It indicate slow-emission, safer indoor air quality, but not a full guarantee of “non-toxic.” For full chemical safety, it's ideal to look for multiple certifications together.

settings.question: What does it mean to be GREENGUARD Gold certified?
settings.answer: It means the product meets stricter emission limits than standard GREENGUARD — especially regarding formaldehyde and VOCs — making it safe for sensitive users like children and the elderly.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD Gold certified mean no PFAS?
settings.answer: GREENGUARD Gold doesn’t explicitly test for PFAS, but products must meet comprehensive VOC emission criteria, which may exclude certain PFAS-related substances.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD Gold mean no fiberglass?
settings.answer: GREENGUARD Gold does not directly address fiberglass. However, if a product contains fiberglass, it must still meet the low emissions criteria for certification.

settings.question: Does GREENGUARD Gold mean no formaldehyde?
settings.answer: It means extremely low formaldehyde emissions. Not entirely “zero,” but well below most safety guidelines — and far lower than what’s found in many conventional products.

settings.question: What does OEKO-TEX® certified mean?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® Standard 100 certified means that a textile product — including every thread, button, and zipper — has been tested for harmful substances and found to be harmless to human health. It's one of the world’s best-known labels for textile safety.

settings.question: What is the purpose of OEKO-TEX® certification?
settings.answer: The purpose is to ensure that textiles are safe for consumers, particularly those with sensitive skin. It protects against exposure to toxic chemicals, allergens, and harmful dyes commonly found in untreated fabrics.

settings.question: What is 100% by OEKO-TEX® certified?
settings.answer: It means the entire product — not just part of it — meets the OEKO-TEX® Standard 100. Every component, from fabric to stitching to labels, is tested for safety and certified.

settings.question: What country is OEKO-TEX® from?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® is based in Switzerland and operates globally through an international network of independent research and testing institutes.

settings.question: How much does it cost to get OEKO-TEX® certified?
settings.answer: The cost varies depending on the product, company size, and testing requirements. It generally involves lab testing fees, annual licensing, and ongoing audits. This investment helps brands like BEDGEAR ensure chemical safety in every product.

settings.question: Is OEKO-TEX® as good as organic?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® focuses on chemical safety, while organic certifications (like GOTS) verify how raw materials are grown. Both are valuable — OEKO-TEX® can apply to both organic and non-organic materials, but ensures no harmful substances are present in the final product.

settings.question: Is organic or OEKO-TEX® better?
settings.answer:
They serve different purposes. 

Organic = farming and environmental practices. 

OEKO-TEX® = product safety for consumers. 

Ideally, a product has both — but OEKO-TEX® ensures non-toxic fabrics regardless of fiber origin.

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® really matter?
settings.answer: Yes. OEKO-TEX® certification is an important consumer safety tool. It ensures that what you’re putting next to your skin, or your child’s skin, is free of hazardous chemicals often found in untreated textiles.

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® mean no chemicals?
settings.answer: Not entirely — it means the product does not contain chemicals at harmful levels. Many safe and necessary treatments (like dyes or finishes) are allowed, but they must pass strict safety thresholds.

settings.question: What is OEKO-TEX® organic?
settings.answer: There’s no such thing as "OEKO-TEX® organic" — however, OEKO-TEX® does certify organic fabrics under its Made in Green and Organic Cotton labels. Standard 100 primarily focuses on chemical safety of the finished product, not how the raw material was farmed.

settings.question: What are the benefits of OEKO-TEX®?
settings.answer:
Safe for sensitive skin and children 

Free from harmful levels of over 100 restricted substances 

Suitable for eco-conscious shoppers 

Tested and verified by independent laboratories 

Peace of mind for your family’s sleep and comfort

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® mean 100% cotton?
settings.answer: No. OEKO-TEX® certification applies to any fabric — natural or synthetic. You can have OEKO-TEX-certified polyester, rayon, cotton, or blends —as long as they pass safety testing.

settings.question: Is OEKO-TEX® made from cotton?
settings.answer: Not necessarily. OEKO-TEX® is a safety certification, not a fabric type. A certified product can be made of cotton, but also of bamboo, microfiber, or synthetics.

settings.question: What are the harmful substances OEKO-TEX® tests for?
settings.answer:
The OEKO-TEX® Standard 100 tests for: 

Formaldehyde 

Heavy metals (like lead, cadmium, arsenic) 

Phthalates 

Pesticide residues 

Chlorinated phenols 

Allergenic dyes 

Azo dyes linked to cancer 

More than 100 substances are evaluated, and limits are updated annually.

settings.question: Is OEKO-TEX® toxic?
settings.answer: No. In fact, OEKO-TEX®-certified products are specifically tested to be non-toxic and skin-safe, making them ideal for babies, children, and those with allergies.

settings.question: What is tested for harmful substances according to OEKO-TEX® Standard?
settings.answer: Everything — fabric, threads, zippers, buttons, foams, coatings, prints, and labels — are individually tested for harmful substances, based on how closely they come into contact with the skin.

settings.question: Is OEKO-TEX® formaldehyde free?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® certification sets extremely low formaldehyde limits, far below most regulatory standards. Many certified products contain no detectable formaldehyde at all.

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® include PFAS?
settings.answer: Yes. As of recent updates, OEKO-TEX® includes PFAS-related substances (also known as “forever chemicals”) in its testing and restrictions, helping reduce long-term chemical exposure.

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® allow BPA?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® places tight restrictions on BPA (Bisphenol A), especially in products intended for infants or direct skin contact. It ensures BPA, if present, is well below health-risk levels.

settings.question: What chemicals are restricted by OEKO-TEX®?
settings.answer:
Restricted groups include: 

Formaldehyde 

Heavy metals 

Azo dyes 

Flame retardants 

PFCs and PFAS 

Phthalates 

Organotin compounds 

It should be noted that the list evolves based on new scientific findings and global regulations.

settings.question: What is CertiPUR-US® certified foam?
settings.answer: CertiPUR-US® certified foam is a type of polyurethane foam that has been independently tested and certified to be made without harmful chemicals, such as ozone depleters, PBDE flame retardants, heavy metals, formaldehyde, or phthalates. It also has low VOC (volatile organic compound) emissions for better indoor air quality.

settings.question: Is CertiPUR-US® certified foam safe?
settings.answer: Yes. CertiPUR-US® certified foam is considered safe for indoor use. It undergoes rigorous lab testing to ensure it doesn’t release hazardous chemicals into your air or skin. It’s a trusted choice for families, kids, and health-conscious buyers.

settings.question: Why are people no longer buying memory foam?
settings.answer:
While memory foam is still popular, some consumers are shifting preferences due to: 

Concerns about heat retention or lack of breathability 

Perceived off-gassing or chemical smells in non-certified foams 

Desire for more eco-friendly or latex-based alternatives 

That said, CertiPUR-US® memory foam addresses many of these concerns, especially when combined with breathable designs like those from BEDGEAR.

settings.question: What does CertiPUR-US® certification mean?
settings.answer:
It means the foam: 

Is made without toxic chemicals 

Emits low VOCs 

Meets durability and performance standards 

Is independently tested for safety and sustainability

settings.question: What is CertiPUR foam made of?
settings.answer:
It’s a type of polyurethane foam, often used in mattresses and pillows, made without: 

Heavy metals (like lead or mercury) 

Formaldehyde 

PBDEs or chlorinated flame retardants 

Phthalates regulated by the Consumer Product Safety Commission

settings.question: Is CertiPUR memory foam safe?
settings.answer: Yes — especially when it’s paired with breathable fabric covers like those from BEDGEAR. CertiPUR memory foam is free from dangerous emissions and is often used in products for children, babies, and those with chemical sensitivities.

settings.question: Does CertiPUR foam contain fiberglass?
settings.answer: No. CertiPUR-US® certification does not require or ban fiberglass, but many certified products, like those from BEDGEAR, avoid using fiberglass for flame resistance and instead opt for safer alternatives.

settings.question: What is CertiPUR memory foam?
settings.answer: It refers to memory foam that has been CertiPUR-US® certified, meaning it retains the classic pressure-relieving feel but is made with safer, cleaner ingredients and low emissions.

settings.question: Is memory foam toxic to the body?
settings.answer: Not when it’s CertiPUR-US® certified. Non-certified memory foam can emit high levels of VOCs or contain unsafe additives, but CertiPUR ensures your foam is non-toxic and safe for daily use.

settings.question: Is CertiPUR foam safe for babies?
settings.answer: Yes — it's one of the safest foams for crib mattresses and baby products, provided it’s used in a well-ventilated, certified design. Always check that the entire product (not just the foam) meets infant safety standards.

settings.question: Do all memory foams have fiberglass?
settings.answer: No. Many newer or premium mattresses — especially those with CertiPUR foam — use alternative fire barriers that are safer and do not rely on fiberglass.

settings.question: What is the healthiest mattress?
settings.answer:
The healthiest mattress is one that is: 

Made with CertiPUR-US® foam 

Free of harmful chemicals and fiberglass 

Breathable and supportive 

Hypoallergenic and resistant to mold, dust mites, and allergens 

At BEDGEAR, we combine certified foam with airflow-enhancing tech and performance fabrics for optimal health and comfort.

settings.question: What is the least toxic foam?
settings.answer: Foams that are CertiPUR-US® certified or made from natural latex are among the least toxic. These options avoid harmful chemicals, off-gassing, and VOCs while still offering comfort and support.

settings.question: What is non-toxic foam?
settings.answer: Non-toxic foam is free from flame retardants, formaldehyde, phthalates, and high-VOC substances. Certifications like CertiPUR-US® or GREENGUARD Gold help confirm this status.

settings.question: Which foam is best for health?
settings.answer: For most sleepers, CertiPUR-US® memory foam or natural latex offer the best balance of comfort, durability, and low chemical exposure.

settings.question: What is the least toxic spray foam?
settings.answer: Low-VOC or water-based spray foams labeled with GreenGuard or similar certifications are the least toxic. However, spray foam is rarely used in bedding — for sleep products, stick to CertiPUR foam.

settings.question: Is all memory foam toxic?
settings.answer: No. While some cheaper memory foams may contain unsafe chemicals, CertiPUR-US® certified memory foam is thoroughly tested to be non-toxic and low in VOCs.

settings.question: Is EVA foam bad for you?
settings.answer: EVA (ethylene vinyl acetate) foam is generally considered safe, but it is not usually CertiPUR-US® certified and may contain trace chemicals if not independently tested. For sleep products, polyurethane foam with certification is usually a better option.

settings.question: What is the healthiest type of mattress?
settings.answer:
A mattress that: 

Uses CertiPUR-US® certified foam 

Is free of fiberglass and harsh flame retardants 

Promotes airflow and spinal alignment 

Avoids allergens and harsh adhesives 

This makes BEDGEAR’s performance mattresses a smart, health-forward choice.

settings.question: Why are people no longer buying memory foam mattresses?
settings.answer:
Some consumers are moving toward hybrid or latex mattresses due to: 

Heat retention in traditional memory foam 

Environmental concerns 

Reports of fiberglass in cheaper models 

But memory foam remains popular, especially when it’s certified, breathable, and combined with other materials — like in BEDGEAR’s advanced sleep systems.

settings.question: Why should you not use a memory foam mattress?
settings.answer:
You might avoid it if: 

You sleep hot on traditional foam 

You’re sensitive to foam smells (non-certified only) 

You want a mattress with more bounce or edge support 

That said, modern memory foam — especially CertiPUR-US® certified — is safe, comfortable, and widely trusted.

settings.question: Are memory foam mattresses still popular?
settings.answer: Yes. Despite new trends, memory foam remains a top choice for pressure relief and contouring support, especially in certified, breathable designs.

settings.question: Should I get rid of my memory foam mattress?
settings.answer:
Only if: 

It’s over 8–10 years old 

It's sagging or no longer supportive 

It contains uncertified or fiberglass-filled foam 

If your memory foam mattress is CertiPUR-US® certified, breathable, and still supportive — you're in good shape.

settings.question: What is the healthiest mattress to sleep on?
settings.answer:
One that’s: 

Free from toxins, fiberglass, and high-VOC chemicals 

Made with CertiPUR-US® or GREENGUARD Gold certified materials 

Supportive for your spine and pressure points 

BEDGEAR’s mattresses check all the boxes for safety, performance, and comfort.

settings.question: Why is memory foam bad for your back?
settings.answer: It’s not — unless it’s too soft or worn out. Poor-quality or sagging foam may lack support, leading to back pain. High-quality memory foam, like what’s in BEDGEAR mattresses, is engineered to contour while supporting healthy alignment.

settings.answer: Everything — fabric, threads, zippers, buttons, foams, coatings, prints, and labels — are individually tested for harmful substances, based on how closely they come into contact with the skin.

settings.question: Is OEKO-TEX® formaldehyde free?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® certification sets extremely low formaldehyde limits, far below most regulatory standards. Many certified products contain no detectable formaldehyde at all.

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® include PFAS?
settings.answer: Yes. As of recent updates, OEKO-TEX® includes PFAS-related substances (also known as “forever chemicals”) in its testing and restrictions, helping reduce long-term chemical exposure.

settings.question: Does OEKO-TEX® allow BPA?
settings.answer: OEKO-TEX® places tight restrictions on BPA (Bisphenol A), especially in products intended for infants or direct skin contact. It ensures BPA, if present, is well below health-risk levels.

settings.question: What chemicals are restricted by OEKO-TEX®?
settings.answer:
Restricted groups include: 

Formaldehyde 

Heavy metals 

Azo dyes 

Flame retardants 

PFCs and PFAS 

Phthalates 

Organotin compounds 

It should be noted that the list evolves based on new scientific findings and global regulations.

settings.question: What is CertiPUR-US® certified foam?
settings.answer: CertiPUR-US® certified foam is a type of polyurethane foam that has been independently tested and certified to be made without harmful chemicals, such as ozone depleters, PBDE flame retardants, heavy metals, formaldehyde, or phthalates. It also has low VOC (volatile organic compound) emissions for better indoor air quality.

settings.question: Is CertiPUR-US® certified foam safe?
settings.answer: Yes. CertiPUR-US® certified foam is considered safe for indoor use. It undergoes rigorous lab testing to ensure it doesn’t release hazardous chemicals into your air or skin. It’s a trusted choice for families, kids, and health-conscious buyers.

settings.question: Why are people no longer buying memory foam?
settings.answer:
While memory foam is still popular, some consumers are shifting preferences due to: 

Concerns about heat retention or lack of breathability 

Perceived off-gassing or chemical smells in non-certified foams 

Desire for more eco-friendly or latex-based alternatives 

That said, CertiPUR-US® memory foam addresses many of these concerns, especially when combined with breathable designs like those from BEDGEAR.

settings.question: What does CertiPUR-US® certification mean?
settings.answer:
It means the foam: 

Is made without toxic chemicals 

Emits low VOCs 

Meets durability and performance standards 

Is independently tested for safety and sustainability

settings.question: What is CertiPUR foam made of?
settings.answer:
It’s a type of polyurethane foam, often used in mattresses and pillows, made without: 

Heavy metals (like lead or mercury) 

Formaldehyde 

PBDEs or chlorinated flame retardants 

Phthalates regulated by the Consumer Product Safety Commission

settings.question: Is CertiPUR memory foam safe?
settings.answer: Yes — especially when it’s paired with breathable fabric covers like those from BEDGEAR. CertiPUR memory foam is free from dangerous emissions and is often used in products for children, babies, and those with chemical sensitivities.

settings.question: Does CertiPUR foam contain fiberglass?
settings.answer: No. CertiPUR-US® certification does not require or ban fiberglass, but many certified products, like those from BEDGEAR, avoid using fiberglass for flame resistance and instead opt for safer alternatives.

settings.question: What is CertiPUR memory foam?
settings.answer: It refers to memory foam that has been CertiPUR-US® certified, meaning it retains the classic pressure-relieving feel but is made with safer, cleaner ingredients and low emissions.

settings.question: Is memory foam toxic to the body?
settings.answer: Not when it’s CertiPUR-US® certified. Non-certified memory foam can emit high levels of VOCs or contain unsafe additives, but CertiPUR ensures your foam is non-toxic and safe for daily use.

settings.question: Is CertiPUR foam safe for babies?
settings.answer: Yes — it's one of the safest foams for crib mattresses and baby products, provided it’s used in a well-ventilated, certified design. Always check that the entire product (not just the foam) meets infant safety standards.

settings.question: Do all memory foams have fiberglass?
settings.answer: No. Many newer or premium mattresses — especially those with CertiPUR foam — use alternative fire barriers that are safer and do not rely on fiberglass.

settings.question: Do all memory foams have fiberglass?
settings.answer: No. Many newer or premium mattresses — especially those with CertiPUR foam — use alternative fire barriers that are safer and do not rely on fiberglass.

settings.question: What is the healthiest mattress?
settings.answer:
The healthiest mattress is one that is: 

Made with CertiPUR-US® foam 

Free of harmful chemicals and fiberglass 

Breathable and supportive 

Hypoallergenic and resistant to mold, dust mites, and allergens 

At BEDGEAR, we combine certified foam with airflow-enhancing tech and performance fabrics for optimal health and comfort.

settings.question: What is the least toxic foam?
settings.answer: Foams that are CertiPUR-US® certified or made from natural latex are among the least toxic. These options avoid harmful chemicals, off-gassing, and VOCs while still offering comfort and support.

settings.question: What is non-toxic foam?
settings.answer: Non-toxic foam is free from flame retardants, formaldehyde, phthalates, and high-VOC substances. Certifications like CertiPUR-US® or GREENGUARD Gold help confirm this status.

settings.question: Which foam is best for health?
settings.answer: For most sleepers, CertiPUR-US® memory foam or natural latex offer the best balance of comfort, durability, and low chemical exposure.

settings.question: What is the least toxic spray foam?
settings.answer: Low-VOC or water-based spray foams labeled with GreenGuard or similar certifications are the least toxic. However, spray foam is rarely used in bedding — for sleep products, stick to CertiPUR foam.

settings.question: Is all memory foam toxic?
settings.answer: No. While some cheaper memory foams may contain unsafe chemicals, CertiPUR-US® certified memory foam is thoroughly tested to be non-toxic and low in VOCs.

settings.question: Is EVA foam bad for you?
settings.answer: EVA (ethylene vinyl acetate) foam is generally considered safe, but it is not usually CertiPUR-US® certified and may contain trace chemicals if not independently tested. For sleep products, polyurethane foam with certification is usually a better option.

settings.question: What is the healthiest type of mattress?
settings.answer:
A mattress that: 

Uses CertiPUR-US® certified foam 

Is free of fiberglass and harsh flame retardants 

Promotes airflow and spinal alignment 

Avoids allergens and harsh adhesives 

This makes BEDGEAR’s performance mattresses a smart, health-forward choice.

settings.question: Why are people no longer buying memory foam mattresses?
settings.answer:
Some consumers are moving toward hybrid or latex mattresses due to: 

Heat retention in traditional memory foam 

Environmental concerns 

Reports of fiberglass in cheaper models 

But memory foam remains popular, especially when it’s certified, breathable, and combined with other materials — like in BEDGEAR’s advanced sleep systems.

settings.question: Why should you not use a memory foam mattress?
settings.answer:
You might avoid it if: 

You sleep hot on traditional foam 

You’re sensitive to foam smells (non-certified only) 

You want a mattress with more bounce or edge support 

That said, modern memory foam — especially CertiPUR-US® certified — is safe, comfortable, and widely trusted.

settings.question: Are memory foam mattresses still popular?
settings.answer: Yes. Despite new trends, memory foam remains a top choice for pressure relief and contouring support, especially in certified, breathable designs.

settings.question: Should I get rid of my memory foam mattress?
settings.answer:
Only if: 

It’s over 8–10 years old 

It's sagging or no longer supportive 

It contains uncertified or fiberglass-filled foam 

If your memory foam mattress is CertiPUR-US® certified, breathable, and still supportive — you're in good shape.

settings.question: What is the healthiest mattress to sleep on?
settings.answer:
One that’s: 

Free from toxins, fiberglass, and high-VOC chemicals 

Made with CertiPUR-US® or GREENGUARD Gold certified materials 

Supportive for your spine and pressure points 

BEDGEAR’s mattresses check all the boxes for safety, performance, and comfort.

settings.question: Why is memory foam bad for your back?
settings.answer: It’s not — unless it’s too soft or worn out. Poor-quality or sagging foam may lack support, leading to back pain. High-quality memory foam, like what’s in BEDGEAR mattresses, is engineered to contour while supporting healthy alignment.
